My wife and I used to measure the speed of a pub in DPH drinks per hour. In my early thirties our local was 2 DPH, which is a fast pub, but as we moved out eight years later it had become a 1.5 DPH (your pint takes 45 mins to drink). The metric started to unravel when we realised that fun atmospheric pubs, problematic flat roof pubs and boring non descript pubs all had a high DPH for different reasons, but we still enjoy pre judging then measuring the DPH of a pub.

Since we’ve had kids most pubs now have a 0 DPH.

Asked about last 7 games and difference in playing teams with something to play for or those in middle of division. Mckenna said biggest challenge is the schedule more so than who you play.

Asked about the scheduling. In the past seasons have been extended to fit in games. Is there a case for that in the modern day.. 
KM I think that would be difficult for the integrity of the competition. For example if we both needed a point us and Southampton could just pass the ball around for 90 mins. 
He thinks there is something that could be done to help Championship teams who go deep in FA CUp and are still going for promotion like Southampton and Coventry last time round.

Asked about Ashley Young.. Back training with the Group. 
Adds to the group every single day he is with us. 
Less cones for Martin to pick up..

Very few players will play all the minutes in the coming games. will need to utilize the strength of the group. 
Jaden had some niggles in the last bit of his return from injury. Got a kick v Millwall which caused him issues. 
He is working hard and is close to being back to where he wants to be. 

Asked about Jens. He is working hard, been a difficult spell for him. Not quite hit the heights he would have hoped to.

Championship is such a tough league to play in. Jens is one who has probably found that out.

I don’t mind this hot take. Leif has the same problem that Trent AA has, in that they started playing football about 20 years after 442 fell out of fashion. He’d be an excellent left midfielder in a 442, able to whip in crosses from the left or a killer through ball, you’re not expected to finish much, and while there’s a need to put your foot in and win the ball back as a midfielder you’ve got a left back behind you better suited to defending.

Obv Kieran won’t play Leif on the wing. In the promotion seasons KMK adapted by - according to the heat maps - telling Leif to GTF away from the defence and join the three attacking midfielders as a line of four behind the forward. When Leif bombed on the right back shuffled over and Morsy and Mass stayed put, giving a screen of two in front of a back three. I’m surprised we weren’t countered more on the wings, it lacks width. Going forwards we have a left winger who cuts in, Jaden/JC, giving Leif the space to overlap. Not sure what’s happened with the early season experiment to have Leif cut inside.

Worst I’d ever seen is South Yorkshire police pre Barnsley, advice was no fans welcome in any pub in Barnsley, and to divert to Wombwell. Spoke to some Barnsley friends who advised it was complete bollocks and that Wombwell is one of the most violent towns in the UK per capita, and to avoid it like the plague. The Barnsley folk fucking love us, always happy to chat and sup a few. I know it’s different tomorrow but.
I'm not convinced that Leif is good enough defensively to play left back in the Premier League, should be fine against Norwich though.

Mass and Burgess were critical to Leif’s best game, we haven’t replaced either adequately enough in my opinion for Leif to do as he did two years ago. I’d start Jaden tomorrow if he’s fully fit because of the cover he can provide as well as his rabbit out of a hat ability going forward.
